name: Catamarca (Argentina)

colors.symbolism: the gold of the Sun of May
purity; the central stripe of the Argentine national flag
the colours of the Argentine flag
the federalist red of caudillo Felipe Varela and the federal heritage of Catamarca
the walnut tree (nogal), characteristic of Catamarca
the trunk and branches of the walnut tree

description: A predominantly white field framed by a thin yellow border above and a wider red band below; an upper-hoist sky-blue canton occupies the inner left quadrant. Centred on the white field is a yellow Sun of May above a brown-and-green walnut branch.
history: Adopted by provincial law 5251 on 25 November 2010 after a public competition won by Mario Manuel Brizuela. The colours combine the celestial blue and white of the Argentine national flag with the federalist red of Catamarcan caudillo Felipe Varela; the walnut branch represents the nogal, a tree closely associated with the cultural identity of Catamarca.

features.symbolism: the celestial blue of the Argentine national flag, recalling the province's place within the Argentine nation
shared with the national flag of Argentina; commemorates the May Revolution of 1810
the walnut tree of Catamarca, traditional source of fine wood and a strong cultural and economic symbol of the province
